aids.title: PNP = Parametric vs Non-Parametric
aids.content: **P**arametric = **P**re-fixed form (Linear, Logistic, Naive Bayes). **N**on-**P**arametric = **N**o fixed form, grows with data (KNN, Decision Trees, SVM).

aids.title: The Blueprint vs Free-Form Sculpture
aids.content: A **parametric model** is like building from a fixed blueprint — the design is decided upfront, you just fill in measurements. A **non-parametric model** is like sculpting clay freely — the shape emerges from the data with no predetermined structure.
aids.forRemembering: Intuition for parametric vs non-parametric models

aids.title: Gradient Descent Memory Aid
aids.content: Think of gradient descent as a ball rolling downhill: the **gradient** tells you which direction is downhill, the **learning rate** controls how big each step is, and the **minimum** of the loss surface is where the ball stops. Write: theta = theta - alpha * gradient.
aids.forRemembering: Gradient descent update rule and intuition
